incorrects.description: Branch 2: Student selected only 7 or only 9 (correct answers only, but not all)

steps.text: You found an odd number. Odd numbers need half-symbols because they don't split evenly into groups of 2. Are there any other odd numbers in the list?

incorrects.description: Branch 3: Student selected only even numbers (all wrong, no correct answers)

steps.text: Even numbers split perfectly into groups of 2. Half-symbols are for numbers that DON'T split evenly. Which numbers leave 1 leftover when you group by 2?

incorrects.description: Branch 4: Student selected mix of odd and even numbers

params.description: Odd numbers in the list pulse briefly.

steps.text: You found the odd numbers. Those DO need half-symbols. But even numbers split perfectly into groups of 2. Which selections don't belong?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: 7 and 9 highlight and pulse to confirm they are the odd numbers.

steps.text: Let me show you. 8 splits evenly: 4 groups of 2. 12 splits evenly: 6 groups of 2. But 7 is 3 groups of 2 plus 1 leftover. 9 is 4 groups of 2 plus 1 leftover. The answers are 7 and 9. Odd numbers need half-symbols because they always have 1 left over.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'Half of the total': misconception that half-symbol relates to total count

steps.text: A half-symbol doesn't show half of the total. It shows half of what ONE symbol is worth. Look at the key. Each symbol equals 2. What is half of 2?

incorrects.description: Student selected '2 items': confused half-symbol with full symbol

steps.text: A full symbol shows 2 items. A HALF-symbol shows half of that. If a full symbol is 2, what is half of 2?

incorrects.description: Student selected 'Nothing—it's decoration': does not understand half-symbols have meaning

steps.text: Half-symbols have real meaning. They show 1 item when the scale is 2. That's how we show odd numbers exactly. What is half of 2?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Key animates: full symbol labeled '2', half-symbol labeled '1'.

steps.text: Here's how it works. The key says each symbol equals 2. So a half-symbol equals half of 2, which is 1. That's how we show the leftover 1 when we have an odd number. The answer is 'Half of 2, which is 1.'
